research.description: DEEP DATA HARVESTER. Crawls multiple websites and extracts full-page text into raw files. Use this ONLY when the user needs massive amounts of raw context for 'NotebookLM' to analyze (e.g., building a dense historical report or technical manual). Overkill for simple questions.

accepts.query: The exact search query string to search for on the web.
accepts.maxPages: (Optional) Integer number of pages to scrape. HARD CAP is 6. Higher values will be ignored.
accepts.sites: (Optional) Array of strings representing specific domains to restrict research to (e.g. ['wikipedia.org', 'reuters.com']).

perplexity.description: FAST ANSWERS & CITATIONS. Uses AI to synthesize a clean, cited summary from live web results. Use this for general queries, quick facts, current news, or 'how-to' questions where deep raw text harvesting is not required.

accepts.query: The research query or question for Perplexity to answer.
accepts.acceptImports: (Optional) boolean. Set to false ONLY if this node should explicitly ignore all incoming upstream files/data. Default true.

gemini.description: Advanced reasoning, text generation, and dynamic analysis. Combines reasoning with data provided from upstream nodes.

accepts.query: The detailed prompt instruction detailing exactly how it should analyze the connected upstream data.
accepts.acceptImports: (Optional) boolean. Set to false ONLY if this node should explicitly ignore all incoming upstream files/data. Default true.

chatgpt.description: Advanced structural reasoning and formatting. Uses OpenAI's ChatGPT. Exceptionally powerful for generating rigorous academic structure, LaTeX code bindings, and highly structured manuals.

accepts.query: The detailed prompt instruction detailing exactly how it should analyze the connected upstream data.
accepts.acceptImports: (Optional) boolean. Set to false ONLY if this node should explicitly ignore all incoming upstream files/data. Default true.

notebooklm.description: Deep QA, complex synthesis, and grounding capabilities strictly tied to files it receives.

accepts.query: The specific question or synthesis command to execute against the context files.
accepts.acceptImports: (Optional) boolean. Set to false ONLY if this node should explicitly ignore all incoming upstream files/data. Default true.

export_file.description: Saves the generated output from upstream nodes into a physical formatted file. ALWAYS append this as the final node if the user explicitly asks to 'generate a .pdf' or 'save to a file'. Supported formats: .pdf, .html, .json, .md, .txt.

accepts.filename: The exact name of the file to save locally, ending in .pdf, .html, .json, .md, or .txt (e.g., 'final_report.html')
